Shrine|WD=Q135039798}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '滋賀県甲賀市水口町嶬峨1606', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '滋賀県甲賀市甲賀町油日', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Yasaka-jinja (Gika, Koka)}} [[JA:八坂神社 (甲賀市)]] {{wikidata link|Q72183108}} == Japanese content == {{神社 | 名称=Yasaka Shrine | 画像=[[File:Yasaka shrine known as Kawakare shrine in Gika, Minakuchi 09.jpg|270px]]
Shrine building | 所在地={{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358}}{{ill|lt=Kōka|Q847584|WD=Q847584}}Minakuchi-chō Gika 1606 | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|57|2.125|N|136|11|48.537|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | 祭神={{ill||lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN}}、{{ill|lt=Emperor Shōmu|Q349263|WD=Q349263}}、{{ill|lt=Emperor Jinmu|Q200188|WD=Q200188}} | 社格={{ill||lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}} candidate shrine, former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} | 創建=(trad.) {{ill|lt=794|Q30887|WD=Q30887}}|例祭={{ill|lt=May 1|Q2544|WD=Q2544}} Kawakare Festival | 本殿=Ikken-sha {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} | 神事={{ill|lt=January 7|Q2228|WD=Q2228}} Matoyama no Okonai | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Shiga|label=Yasaka Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Yasaka Shrine''' (やさかじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in Gika, Minakuchi-chō, {{ill|lt=Kōka|Q847584|WD=Q847584}}, {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358}}. === History === According to shrine tradition, Kawakare deity, ancestral deity of local development, was invited here. In {{ill|lt=794|Q30887|WD=Q30887}} (approximately {{ill|lt=Tenpyō|Q1199879|WD=Q1199879}} 21), when Left Minister {{ill|lt=Tachibana no Moroe|Q3135863|WD=Q3135863}} built temple {{ill|lt=garan|Q3482038|WD=Q3482038}} at neighboring {{ill|lt=Senkō-ji (Kōka)|Q72184313|WD=Q72184313|lt=Senkō-ji}}, shrine buildings were built to protect Buddhist law{{Cite web|和書|title=Shrine introduction > Yasaka Shrine (Yasaka) ~ (Saga Ōmiya)|url=http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=556|accessdate=2019-09-30|publisher=Shiga Prefectural Shrine Association}}. In {{ill|lt=932|Q23656|WD=Q23656}} ({{ill|lt=Jōhei (Japan)|Q1324874|WD=Q1324874|lt=Jōhei}} 2), for distribution of divine virtue, it received the title Giga Ōmiya and shrine buildings were erected at the current site. The honden was built in {{ill|lt=1439|Q6380|WD=Q6380}} ({{ill|lt=Eikyō|Q1193059|WD=Q1193059}} 11). According to Giga Ōmiya Senkō-ji records, the honden was rebuilt in {{ill|lt=1587|Q6744|WD=Q6744}} ({{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058}} 15), matching architectural style and methods. After repairs in {{ill|lt=1721|Q6913|WD=Q6913}} ({{ill|lt=Kyōhō|Q1145048|WD=Q1145048}} 6) and {{ill|lt=1866|Q7712|WD=Q7712}} ({{ill|lt=Keiō|Q776775|WD=Q776775}} 2), dismantling and restoration were performed in {{ill|lt=1959|Q5302|WD=Q5302}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567}} 34). In the {{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292}} it was called Kawakare Shrine, later Gika Ōmiya, then Gozu Tennō Shrine, and now Yasaka Shrine{{Cite book|title=Kōka City History Vol.8 Kōka City Encyclopedia|date=2016-12-12|year=2016|publisher=Kōka City|page=49}}. === Enshrined deities === {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Emperor Shōmu|Q349263|WD=Q349263}}, {{ill|lt=Emperor Jinmu|Q200188|WD=Q200188}} === Festivals === * Kawakare Festival - {{ill|lt=May 1|Q2544|WD=Q2544}} ** Day before - first-age yakudoshi men carry lion head and visit all parishioner households ** Day of festival - rites in morning; from afternoon mikoshi procession (60 youths in Genroku-style costumes cross the Yasu River) * Matoyama no Okonai - {{ill|lt=January 7|Q2228|WD=Q2228}} ** As a miyaza rite, prayer for health/longevity of eldest son and family prosperity, etc. Viewing not permitted. === Precinct shrines === * Tenjin Shrine * Kumano Shrine * Kawakare Shrine * Hachiman Shrine * Yasaka Shrine === Cultural properties === === Important Cultural Property (National designation) === * Honden - ikken-sha nagare-zukuri, cypress-bark roof === City designated cultural properties === * Sacred images - made in Heian period *Gebabashi bridge - offered by parishioners in 1699 (Genroku 12) in thanks for successful rain prayer. Elaborate arched bridge. === Precinct === ファイル:Torii, Yasaka shrine known as Kawakare shrine in Gika, Minakuchi.jpg|Torii ファイル:Yasaka shrine known as Kawakare shrine in Gika, Minakuchi 03.jpg|Near stone steps ファイル:Stone bridge at Yasaka shrine known as Kawakare shrine in Gika, Minakuchi 01.jpg|Gebabashi bridge ファイル:Yasaka shrine known as Kawakare shrine in Gika, Minakuchi 04.jpg|Haiden and honden ファイル:Yasaka shrine known as Kawakare shrine in Gika, Minakuchi 07.jpg|Honden ファイル:Cryptomeria japonica at Yasaka shrine known as Kawakare shrine in Gika, Minakuchi.jpg|Great cedar === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} {{commons|Category:Yasaka-jinja (Gika, Koka)}} {{DEFAULTSORT:やさかしんしや こうかし}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Yasaka Shrine (Q72183108)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Yasaka Shrine (Q72183108)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Yasaka Shrine (Q72183108)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:八坂神社]] [[Category:近江国の式内社]] [[Category:滋賀県の旧村社]] [[Category:滋賀県の神社]] [[Category:甲賀市の建築物]] [[Category:甲賀市の歴史]] [[Category:甲賀市の重要文化財]] [[Category:重要文化財 (建造物)]] [[Category:滋賀県内の市町村指定有形文化財]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QYasaka Shrine (Q72183108)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QYasaka Shrine (Q72183108)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Yasaka Shrine (Q72183108)}}[[Category:qqq]]

Shrine|WD=Q135039798}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '滋賀県甲賀市水口町嶬峨1606', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '滋賀県甲賀市甲賀町油日', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Yasaka-jinja (Gika, Koka)}} [[JA:八坂神社 (甲賀市)]] {{wikidata link|Q72183108}} == Japanese content == {{神社 | 名称=Yasaka Shrine | 画像=[[File:Yasaka shrine known as Kawakare shrine in Gika, Minakuchi 09.jpg|270px]]
Shrine building | 所在地={{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358}}{{ill|lt=Kōka|Q847584|WD=Q847584}}Minakuchi-chō Gika 1606 | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|57|2.125|N|136|11|48.537|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | 祭神={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN}}、{{ill|lt=Emperor Shōmu|Q349263|WD=Q349263}}、{{ill|lt=Emperor Jinmu|Q200188|WD=Q200188}} | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}} candidate shrine, former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} | 創建=(trad.) {{ill|lt=794|Q30887|WD=Q30887}}|例祭={{ill|lt=May 1|Q2544|WD=Q2544}} Kawakare Festival | 本殿=Ikken-sha {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} | 神事={{ill|lt=January 7|Q2228|WD=Q2228}} Matoyama no Okonai | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Shiga|label=Yasaka Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Yasaka Shrine''' (やさかじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in Gika, Minakuchi-chō, {{ill|lt=Kōka|Q847584|WD=Q847584}}, {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358}}. === History === According to shrine tradition, Kawakare deity, ancestral deity of local development, was invited here. In {{ill|lt=794|Q30887|WD=Q30887}} (approximately {{ill|lt=Tenpyō|Q1199879|WD=Q1199879}} 21), when Left Minister {{ill|lt=Tachibana no Moroe|Q3135863|WD=Q3135863}} built temple {{ill|lt=garan|Q3482038|WD=Q3482038}} at neighboring {{ill|lt=Senkō-ji (Kōka)|Q72184313|WD=Q72184313|lt=Senkō-ji}}, shrine buildings were built to protect Buddhist law{{Cite web|和書|title=Shrine introduction > Yasaka Shrine (Yasaka) ~ (Saga Ōmiya)|url=http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=556|accessdate=2019-09-30|publisher=Shiga Prefectural Shrine Association}}. In {{ill|lt=932|Q23656|WD=Q23656}} ({{ill|lt=Jōhei (Japan)|Q1324874|WD=Q1324874|lt=Jōhei}} 2), for distribution of divine virtue, it received the title Giga Ōmiya and shrine buildings were erected at the current site. The honden was built in {{ill|lt=1439|Q6380|WD=Q6380}} ({{ill|lt=Eikyō|Q1193059|WD=Q1193059}} 11). According to Giga Ōmiya Senkō-ji records, the honden was rebuilt in {{ill|lt=1587|Q6744|WD=Q6744}} ({{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058}} 15), matching architectural style and methods. After repairs in {{ill|lt=1721|Q6913|WD=Q6913}} ({{ill|lt=Kyōhō|Q1145048|WD=Q1145048}} 6) and {{ill|lt=1866|Q7712|WD=Q7712}} ({{ill|lt=Keiō|Q776775|WD=Q776775}} 2), dismantling and restoration were performed in {{ill|lt=1959|Q5302|WD=Q5302}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567}} 34). In the {{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292}} it was called Kawakare Shrine, later Gika Ōmiya, then Gozu Tennō Shrine, and now Yasaka Shrine{{Cite book|title=Kōka City History Vol.8 Kōka City Encyclopedia|date=2016-12-12|year=2016|publisher=Kōka City|page=49}}. === Enshrined deities === {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Emperor Shōmu|Q349263|WD=Q349263}}, {{ill|lt=Emperor Jinmu|Q200188|WD=Q200188}} === Festivals === * Kawakare Festival - {{ill|lt=May 1|Q2544|WD=Q2544}} ** Day before - first-age yakudoshi men carry lion head and visit all parishioner households ** Day of festival - rites in morning; from afternoon mikoshi procession (60 youths in Genroku-style costumes cross the Yasu River) * Matoyama no Okonai - {{ill|lt=January 7|Q2228|WD=Q2228}} ** As a miyaza rite, prayer for health/longevity of eldest son and family prosperity, etc. Viewing not permitted. === Precinct shrines === * Tenjin Shrine * Kumano Shrine * Kawakare Shrine * Hachiman Shrine * Yasaka Shrine === Cultural properties === === Important Cultural Property (National designation) === * Honden - ikken-sha nagare-zukuri, cypress-bark roof === City designated cultural properties === * Sacred images - made in Heian period *Gebabashi bridge - offered by parishioners in 1699 (Genroku 12) in thanks for successful rain prayer. Elaborate arched bridge. === Precinct === ファイル:Torii, Yasaka shrine known as Kawakare shrine in Gika, Minakuchi.jpg|Torii ファイル:Yasaka shrine known as Kawakare shrine in Gika, Minakuchi 03.jpg|Near stone steps ファイル:Stone bridge at Yasaka shrine known as Kawakare shrine in Gika, Minakuchi 01.jpg|Gebabashi bridge ファイル:Yasaka shrine known as Kawakare shrine in Gika, Minakuchi 04.jpg|Haiden and honden ファイル:Yasaka shrine known as Kawakare shrine in Gika, Minakuchi 07.jpg|Honden ファイル:Cryptomeria japonica at Yasaka shrine known as Kawakare shrine in Gika, Minakuchi.jpg|Great cedar === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} {{commons|Category:Yasaka-jinja (Gika, Koka)}} {{DEFAULTSORT:やさかしんしや こうかし}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Yasaka Shrine (Q72183108)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Yasaka Shrine (Q72183108)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Yasaka Shrine (Q72183108)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:八坂神社]] [[Category:近江国の式内社]] [[Category:滋賀県の旧村社]] [[Category:滋賀県の神社]] [[Category:甲賀市の建築物]] [[Category:甲賀市の歴史]] [[Category:甲賀市の重要文化財]] [[Category:重要文化財 (建造物)]] [[Category:滋賀県内の市町村指定有形文化財]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QYasaka Shrine (Q72183108)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QYasaka Shrine (Q72183108)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Yasaka Shrine (Q72183108)}}[[Category:qqq]]

Shrine|WD=Q135039798}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '滋賀県甲賀市水口町嶬峨1606', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '滋賀県甲賀市甲賀町油日', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Yasaka-jinja (Gika, Koka)}} [[JA:八坂神社 (甲賀市)]] {{wikidata link|Q72183108}} == Japanese content == {{神社 | 名称=Yasaka Shrine | 画像=[[File:Yasaka shrine known as Kawakare shrine in Gika, Minakuchi 09.jpg|270px]]
Shrine building | 所在地={{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358}}{{ill|lt=Kōka|Q847584|WD=Q847584}}Minakuchi-chō Gika 1606 | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|57|2.125|N|136|11|48.537|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | 祭神={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}、{{ill|lt=Emperor Shōmu|Q349263|WD=Q349263}}、{{ill|lt=Emperor Jinmu|Q200188|WD=Q200188}} | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}} candidate shrine, former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} | 創建=(trad.) {{ill|lt=794|Q30887|WD=Q30887}}|例祭={{ill|lt=May 1|Q2544|WD=Q2544}} Kawakare Festival | 本殿=Ikken-sha {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} | 神事={{ill|lt=January 7|Q2228|WD=Q2228}} Matoyama no Okonai | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Shiga|label=Yasaka Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Yasaka Shrine''' (やさかじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in Gika, Minakuchi-chō, {{ill|lt=Kōka|Q847584|WD=Q847584}}, {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358}}. === History === According to shrine tradition, Kawakare deity, ancestral deity of local development, was invited here. In {{ill|lt=794|Q30887|WD=Q30887}} (approximately {{ill|lt=Tenpyō|Q1199879|WD=Q1199879}} 21), when Left Minister {{ill|lt=Tachibana no Moroe|Q3135863|WD=Q3135863}} built temple {{ill|lt=garan|Q3482038|WD=Q3482038}} at neighboring {{ill|lt=Senkō-ji (Kōka)|Q72184313|WD=Q72184313|lt=Senkō-ji}}, shrine buildings were built to protect Buddhist law{{Cite web|和書|title=Shrine introduction > Yasaka Shrine (Yasaka) ~ (Saga Ōmiya)|url=http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=556|accessdate=2019-09-30|publisher=Shiga Prefectural Shrine Association}}. In {{ill|lt=932|Q23656|WD=Q23656}} ({{ill|lt=Jōhei (Japan)|Q1324874|WD=Q1324874|lt=Jōhei}} 2), for distribution of divine virtue, it received the title Giga Ōmiya and shrine buildings were erected at the current site. The honden was built in {{ill|lt=1439|Q6380|WD=Q6380}} ({{ill|lt=Eikyō|Q1193059|WD=Q1193059}} 11). According to Giga Ōmiya Senkō-ji records, the honden was rebuilt in {{ill|lt=1587|Q6744|WD=Q6744}} ({{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058}} 15), matching architectural style and methods. After repairs in {{ill|lt=1721|Q6913|WD=Q6913}} ({{ill|lt=Kyōhō|Q1145048|WD=Q1145048}} 6) and {{ill|lt=1866|Q7712|WD=Q7712}} ({{ill|lt=Keiō|Q776775|WD=Q776775}} 2), dismantling and restoration were performed in {{ill|lt=1959|Q5302|WD=Q5302}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567}} 34). In the {{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292}} it was called Kawakare Shrine, later Gika Ōmiya, then Gozu Tennō Shrine, and now Yasaka Shrine{{Cite book|title=Kōka City History Vol.8 Kōka City Encyclopedia|date=2016-12-12|year=2016|publisher=Kōka City|page=49}}. === Enshrined deities === {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Emperor Shōmu|Q349263|WD=Q349263}}, {{ill|lt=Emperor Jinmu|Q200188|WD=Q200188}} === Festivals === * Kawakare Festival - {{ill|lt=May 1|Q2544|WD=Q2544}} ** Day before - first-age yakudoshi men carry lion head and visit all parishioner households ** Day of festival - rites in morning; from afternoon mikoshi procession (60 youths in Genroku-style costumes cross the Yasu River) * Matoyama no Okonai - {{ill|lt=January 7|Q2228|WD=Q2228}} ** As a miyaza rite, prayer for health/longevity of eldest son and family prosperity, etc. Viewing not permitted. === Precinct shrines === * Tenjin Shrine * Kumano Shrine * Kawakare Shrine * Hachiman Shrine * Yasaka Shrine === Cultural properties === === Important Cultural Property (National designation) === * Honden - ikken-sha nagare-zukuri, cypress-bark roof === City designated cultural properties === * Sacred images - made in Heian period *Gebabashi bridge - offered by parishioners in 1699 (Genroku 12) in thanks for successful rain prayer. Elaborate arched bridge. === Precinct === ファイル:Torii, Yasaka shrine known as Kawakare shrine in Gika, Minakuchi.jpg|Torii ファイル:Yasaka shrine known as Kawakare shrine in Gika, Minakuchi 03.jpg|Near stone steps ファイル:Stone bridge at Yasaka shrine known as Kawakare shrine in Gika, Minakuchi 01.jpg|Gebabashi bridge ファイル:Yasaka shrine known as Kawakare shrine in Gika, Minakuchi 04.jpg|Haiden and honden ファイル:Yasaka shrine known as Kawakare shrine in Gika, Minakuchi 07.jpg|Honden ファイル:Cryptomeria japonica at Yasaka shrine known as Kawakare shrine in Gika, Minakuchi.jpg|Great cedar === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} {{commons|Category:Yasaka-jinja (Gika, Koka)}} {{DEFAULTSORT:やさかしんしや こうかし}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Yasaka Shrine (Q72183108)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Yasaka Shrine (Q72183108)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Yasaka Shrine (Q72183108)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:八坂神社]] [[Category:近江国の式内社]] [[Category:滋賀県の旧村社]] [[Category:滋賀県の神社]] [[Category:甲賀市の建築物]] [[Category:甲賀市の歴史]] [[Category:甲賀市の重要文化財]] [[Category:重要文化財 (建造物)]] [[Category:滋賀県内の市町村指定有形文化財]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QYasaka Shrine (Q72183108)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QYasaka Shrine (Q72183108)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Yasaka Shrine (Q72183108)}}[[Category:qqq]]

Shrine|WD=Q135039798}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '滋賀県甲賀市水口町嶬峨1606', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '滋賀県甲賀市甲賀町油日', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Yasaka-jinja (Gika, Koka)}} [[JA:八坂神社 (甲賀市)]] {{wikidata link|Q72183108}} == Japanese content == {{神社 | 名称=Yasaka Shrine | 画像=[[File:Yasaka shrine known as Kawakare shrine in Gika, Minakuchi 09.jpg|270px]]
Shrine building | 所在地={{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358}}{{ill|lt=Kōka|Q847584|WD=Q847584}}Minakuchi-chō Gika 1606 | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|57|2.125|N|136|11|48.537|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | 祭神={{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993}}、{{ill|lt=Emperor Shōmu|Q349263|WD=Q349263}}、{{ill|lt=Emperor Jinmu|Q200188|WD=Q200188}} | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}} candidate shrine, former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} | 創建=(trad.) {{ill|lt=794|Q30887|WD=Q30887}}|例祭={{ill|lt=May 1|Q2544|WD=Q2544}} Kawakare Festival | 本殿=Ikken-sha {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} | 神事={{ill|lt=January 7|Q2228|WD=Q2228}} Matoyama no Okonai | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Shiga|label=Yasaka Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Yasaka Shrine''' (やさかじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in Gika, Minakuchi-chō, {{ill|lt=Kōka|Q847584|WD=Q847584}}, {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358}}. === History === According to shrine tradition, Kawakare deity, ancestral deity of local development, was invited here. In {{ill|lt=794|Q30887|WD=Q30887}} (approximately {{ill|lt=Tenpyō|Q1199879|WD=Q1199879}} 21), when Left Minister {{ill|lt=Tachibana no Moroe|Q3135863|WD=Q3135863}} built temple {{ill|lt=garan|Q3482038|WD=Q3482038}} at neighboring {{ill|lt=Senkō-ji (Kōka)|Q72184313|WD=Q72184313|lt=Senkō-ji}}, shrine buildings were built to protect Buddhist law{{Cite web|和書|title=Shrine introduction > Yasaka Shrine (Yasaka) ~ (Saga Ōmiya)|url=http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=556|accessdate=2019-09-30|publisher=Shiga Prefectural Shrine Association}}. In {{ill|lt=932|Q23656|WD=Q23656}} ({{ill|lt=Jōhei (Japan)|Q1324874|WD=Q1324874|lt=Jōhei}} 2), for distribution of divine virtue, it received the title Giga Ōmiya and shrine buildings were erected at the current site. The honden was built in {{ill|lt=1439|Q6380|WD=Q6380}} ({{ill|lt=Eikyō|Q1193059|WD=Q1193059}} 11). According to Giga Ōmiya Senkō-ji records, the honden was rebuilt in {{ill|lt=1587|Q6744|WD=Q6744}} ({{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058}} 15), matching architectural style and methods. After repairs in {{ill|lt=1721|Q6913|WD=Q6913}} ({{ill|lt=Kyōhō|Q1145048|WD=Q1145048}} 6) and {{ill|lt=1866|Q7712|WD=Q7712}} ({{ill|lt=Keiō|Q776775|WD=Q776775}} 2), dismantling and restoration were performed in {{ill|lt=1959|Q5302|WD=Q5302}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567}} 34). In the {{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292}} it was called Kawakare Shrine, later Gika Ōmiya, then Gozu Tennō Shrine, and now Yasaka Shrine{{Cite book|title=Kōka City History Vol.8 Kōka City Encyclopedia|date=2016-12-12|year=2016|publisher=Kōka City|page=49}}. === Enshrined deities === {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Emperor Shōmu|Q349263|WD=Q349263}}, {{ill|lt=Emperor Jinmu|Q200188|WD=Q200188}} === Festivals === * Kawakare Festival - {{ill|lt=May 1|Q2544|WD=Q2544}} ** Day before - first-age yakudoshi men carry lion head and visit all parishioner households ** Day of festival - rites in morning; from afternoon mikoshi procession (60 youths in Genroku-style costumes cross the Yasu River) * Matoyama no Okonai - {{ill|lt=January 7|Q2228|WD=Q2228}} ** As a miyaza rite, prayer for health/longevity of eldest son and family prosperity, etc. Viewing not permitted. === Precinct shrines === * Tenjin Shrine * Kumano Shrine * Kawakare Shrine * Hachiman Shrine * Yasaka Shrine === Cultural properties === === Important Cultural Property (National designation) === * Honden - ikken-sha nagare-zukuri, cypress-bark roof === City designated cultural properties === * Sacred images - made in Heian period *Gebabashi bridge - offered by parishioners in 1699 (Genroku 12) in thanks for successful rain prayer. Elaborate arched bridge. === Precinct === ファイル:Torii, Yasaka shrine known as Kawakare shrine in Gika, Minakuchi.jpg|Torii ファイル:Yasaka shrine known as Kawakare shrine in Gika, Minakuchi 03.jpg|Near stone steps ファイル:Stone bridge at Yasaka shrine known as Kawakare shrine in Gika, Minakuchi 01.jpg|Gebabashi bridge ファイル:Yasaka shrine known as Kawakare shrine in Gika, Minakuchi 04.jpg|Haiden and honden ファイル:Yasaka shrine known as Kawakare shrine in Gika, Minakuchi 07.jpg|Honden ファイル:Cryptomeria japonica at Yasaka shrine known as Kawakare shrine in Gika, Minakuchi.jpg|Great cedar === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} {{commons|Category:Yasaka-jinja (Gika, Koka)}} {{DEFAULTSORT:やさかしんしや こうかし}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Yasaka Shrine (Q72183108)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Yasaka Shrine (Q72183108)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Yasaka Shrine (Q72183108)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:八坂神社]] [[Category:近江国の式内社]] [[Category:滋賀県の旧村社]] [[Category:滋賀県の神社]] [[Category:甲賀市の建築物]] [[Category:甲賀市の歴史]] [[Category:甲賀市の重要文化財]] [[Category:重要文化財 (建造物)]] [[Category:滋賀県内の市町村指定有形文化財]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QYasaka Shrine (Q72183108)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QYasaka Shrine (Q72183108)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Yasaka Shrine (Q72183108)}}[[Category:qqq]]

Shrine|WD=Q135039798}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '滋賀県甲賀市水口町嶬峨1606', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '滋賀県甲賀市甲賀町油日', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Yasaka-jinja (Gika, Koka)}} [[JA:八坂神社 (甲賀市)]] {{wikidata link|Q72183108}} == Japanese content == {{神社 | 名称=Yasaka Shrine | 画像=[[File:Yasaka shrine known as Kawakare shrine in Gika, Minakuchi 09.jpg|270px]]
Shrine building | 所在地={{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358}}{{ill|lt=Kōka|Q847584|WD=Q847584}}Minakuchi-chō Gika 1606 | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|57|2.125|N|136|11|48.537|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | 祭神={{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993}}、{{ill|lt=Emperor Shōmu|Q349263|WD=Q349263}}、{{ill|lt=Emperor Jinmu|Q200188|WD=Q200188}} | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} candidate shrine, former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} | 創建=(trad.) {{ill|lt=794|Q30887|WD=Q30887}}|例祭={{ill|lt=May 1|Q2544|WD=Q2544}} Kawakare Festival | 本殿=Ikken-sha {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} | 神事={{ill|lt=January 7|Q2228|WD=Q2228}} Matoyama no Okonai | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Shiga|label=Yasaka Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Yasaka Shrine''' (やさかじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in Gika, Minakuchi-chō, {{ill|lt=Kōka|Q847584|WD=Q847584}}, {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358}}. === History === According to shrine tradition, Kawakare deity, ancestral deity of local development, was invited here. In {{ill|lt=794|Q30887|WD=Q30887}} (approximately {{ill|lt=Tenpyō|Q1199879|WD=Q1199879}} 21), when Left Minister {{ill|lt=Tachibana no Moroe|Q3135863|WD=Q3135863}} built temple {{ill|lt=garan|Q3482038|WD=Q3482038}} at neighboring {{ill|lt=Senkō-ji (Kōka)|Q72184313|WD=Q72184313|lt=Senkō-ji}}, shrine buildings were built to protect Buddhist law{{Cite web|和書|title=Shrine introduction > Yasaka Shrine (Yasaka) ~ (Saga Ōmiya)|url=http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=556|accessdate=2019-09-30|publisher=Shiga Prefectural Shrine Association}}. In {{ill|lt=932|Q23656|WD=Q23656}} ({{ill|lt=Jōhei (Japan)|Q1324874|WD=Q1324874|lt=Jōhei}} 2), for distribution of divine virtue, it received the title Giga Ōmiya and shrine buildings were erected at the current site. The honden was built in {{ill|lt=1439|Q6380|WD=Q6380}} ({{ill|lt=Eikyō|Q1193059|WD=Q1193059}} 11). According to Giga Ōmiya Senkō-ji records, the honden was rebuilt in {{ill|lt=1587|Q6744|WD=Q6744}} ({{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058}} 15), matching architectural style and methods. After repairs in {{ill|lt=1721|Q6913|WD=Q6913}} ({{ill|lt=Kyōhō|Q1145048|WD=Q1145048}} 6) and {{ill|lt=1866|Q7712|WD=Q7712}} ({{ill|lt=Keiō|Q776775|WD=Q776775}} 2), dismantling and restoration were performed in {{ill|lt=1959|Q5302|WD=Q5302}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567}} 34). In the {{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292}} it was called Kawakare Shrine, later Gika Ōmiya, then Gozu Tennō Shrine, and now Yasaka Shrine{{Cite book|title=Kōka City History Vol.8 Kōka City Encyclopedia|date=2016-12-12|year=2016|publisher=Kōka City|page=49}}. === Enshrined deities === {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Emperor Shōmu|Q349263|WD=Q349263}}, {{ill|lt=Emperor Jinmu|Q200188|WD=Q200188}} === Festivals === * Kawakare Festival - {{ill|lt=May 1|Q2544|WD=Q2544}} ** Day before - first-age yakudoshi men carry lion head and visit all parishioner households ** Day of festival - rites in morning; from afternoon mikoshi procession (60 youths in Genroku-style costumes cross the Yasu River) * Matoyama no Okonai - {{ill|lt=January 7|Q2228|WD=Q2228}} ** As a miyaza rite, prayer for health/longevity of eldest son and family prosperity, etc. Viewing not permitted. === Precinct shrines === * Tenjin Shrine * Kumano Shrine * Kawakare Shrine * Hachiman Shrine * Yasaka Shrine === Cultural properties === === Important Cultural Property (National designation) === * Honden - ikken-sha nagare-zukuri, cypress-bark roof === City designated cultural properties === * Sacred images - made in Heian period *Gebabashi bridge - offered by parishioners in 1699 (Genroku 12) in thanks for successful rain prayer. Elaborate arched bridge. === Precinct === ファイル:Torii, Yasaka shrine known as Kawakare shrine in Gika, Minakuchi.jpg|Torii ファイル:Yasaka shrine known as Kawakare shrine in Gika, Minakuchi 03.jpg|Near stone steps ファイル:Stone bridge at Yasaka shrine known as Kawakare shrine in Gika, Minakuchi 01.jpg|Gebabashi bridge ファイル:Yasaka shrine known as Kawakare shrine in Gika, Minakuchi 04.jpg|Haiden and honden ファイル:Yasaka shrine known as Kawakare shrine in Gika, Minakuchi 07.jpg|Honden ファイル:Cryptomeria japonica at Yasaka shrine known as Kawakare shrine in Gika, Minakuchi.jpg|Great cedar === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} {{commons|Category:Yasaka-jinja (Gika, Koka)}} {{DEFAULTSORT:やさかしんしや こうかし}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Yasaka Shrine (Q72183108)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Yasaka Shrine (Q72183108)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Yasaka Shrine (Q72183108)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:八坂神社]] [[Category:近江国の式内社]] [[Category:滋賀県の旧村社]] [[Category:滋賀県の神社]] [[Category:甲賀市の建築物]] [[Category:甲賀市の歴史]] [[Category:甲賀市の重要文化財]] [[Category:重要文化財 (建造物)]] [[Category:滋賀県内の市町村指定有形文化財]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QYasaka Shrine (Q72183108)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QYasaka Shrine (Q72183108)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Yasaka Shrine (Q72183108)}}[[Category:qqq]]

Shrine|WD=Q135039798}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '滋賀県甲賀市水口町嶬峨1606', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '滋賀県甲賀市甲賀町油日', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Yasaka-jinja (Gika, Koka)}} [[JA:八坂神社 (甲賀市)]] {{wikidata link|Q72183108}} == Japanese content == {{神社 | 名称=Yasaka Shrine | 画像=[[File:Yasaka shrine known as Kawakare shrine in Gika, Minakuchi 09.jpg|270px]]
Shrine building | 所在地={{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358}}{{ill|lt=Kōka|Q847584|WD=Q847584}}Minakuchi-chō Gika 1606 | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|57|2.125|N|136|11|48.537|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | 祭神={{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993}}、{{ill|lt=Emperor Shōmu|Q349263|WD=Q349263}}、{{ill|lt=Emperor Jinmu|Q200188|WD=Q200188}} | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} candidate shrine, former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} | 創建=(trad.) {{ill|lt=794|Q30887|WD=Q30887}}|例祭={{ill|lt=May 1|Q2544|WD=Q2544}} Kawakare Festival | 本殿=Ikken-sha {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} | 神事={{ill|lt=January 7|Q2228|WD=Q2228}} Matoyama no Okonai | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Shiga|label=Yasaka Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Yasaka Shrine''' (やさかじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in Gika, Minakuchi-chō, {{ill|lt=Kōka|Q847584|WD=Q847584}}, {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358}}. === History === According to shrine tradition, Kawakare deity, ancestral deity of local development, was invited here. In {{ill|lt=794|Q30887|WD=Q30887}} (approximately {{ill|lt=Tenpyō|Q1199879|WD=Q1199879}} 21), when Left Minister {{ill|lt=Tachibana no Moroe|Q3135863|WD=Q3135863}} built temple {{ill|lt=garan|Q3482038|WD=Q3482038}} at neighboring {{ill|lt=Senkō-ji (Kōka)|Q72184313|WD=Q72184313|lt=Senkō-ji}}, shrine buildings were built to protect Buddhist law{{Cite web|和書|title=Shrine introduction > Yasaka Shrine (Yasaka) ~ (Saga Ōmiya)|url=http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=556|accessdate=2019-09-30|publisher=Shiga Prefectural Shrine Association}}. In {{ill|lt=932|Q23656|WD=Q23656}} ({{ill|lt=Jōhei (Japan)|Q1324874|WD=Q1324874|lt=Jōhei}} 2), for distribution of divine virtue, it received the title Giga Ōmiya and shrine buildings were erected at the current site. The honden was built in {{ill|lt=1439|Q6380|WD=Q6380}} ({{ill|lt=Eikyō|Q1193059|WD=Q1193059}} 11). According to Giga Ōmiya Senkō-ji records, the honden was rebuilt in {{ill|lt=1587|Q6744|WD=Q6744}} ({{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058}} 15), matching architectural style and methods. After repairs in {{ill|lt=1721|Q6913|WD=Q6913}} ({{ill|lt=Kyōhō|Q1145048|WD=Q1145048}} 6) and {{ill|lt=1866|Q7712|WD=Q7712}} ({{ill|lt=Keiō|Q776775|WD=Q776775}} 2), dismantling and restoration were performed in {{ill|lt=1959|Q5302|WD=Q5302}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567}} 34). In the {{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292}} it was called Kawakare Shrine, later Gika Ōmiya, then Gozu Tennō Shrine, and now Yasaka Shrine{{Cite book|title=Kōka City History Vol.8 Kōka City Encyclopedia|date=2016-12-12|year=2016|publisher=Kōka City|page=49}}. === Enshrined deities === {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Emperor Shōmu|Q349263|WD=Q349263}}, {{ill|lt=Emperor Jinmu|Q200188|WD=Q200188}} === Festivals === * Kawakare Festival - {{ill|lt=May 1|Q2544|WD=Q2544}} ** Day before - first-age yakudoshi men carry lion head and visit all parishioner households ** Day of festival - rites in morning; from afternoon mikoshi procession (60 youths in Genroku-style costumes cross the Yasu River) * Matoyama no Okonai - {{ill|lt=January 7|Q2228|WD=Q2228}} ** As a miyaza rite, prayer for health/longevity of eldest son and family prosperity, etc. Viewing not permitted. === Precinct shrines === * Tenjin Shrine * Kumano Shrine * Kawakare Shrine * Hachiman Shrine * Yasaka Shrine === Cultural properties === === Important Cultural Property (National designation) === * Honden - ikken-sha nagare-zukuri, cypress-bark roof === City designated cultural properties === * Sacred images - made in Heian period *Gebabashi bridge - offered by parishioners in 1699 (Genroku 12) in thanks for successful rain prayer. Elaborate arched bridge. === Precinct === ファイル:Torii, Yasaka shrine known as Kawakare shrine in Gika, Minakuchi.jpg|Torii ファイル:Yasaka shrine known as Kawakare shrine in Gika, Minakuchi 03.jpg|Near stone steps ファイル:Stone bridge at Yasaka shrine known as Kawakare shrine in Gika, Minakuchi 01.jpg|Gebabashi bridge ファイル:Yasaka shrine known as Kawakare shrine in Gika, Minakuchi 04.jpg|Haiden and honden ファイル:Yasaka shrine known as Kawakare shrine in Gika, Minakuchi 07.jpg|Honden ファイル:Cryptomeria japonica at Yasaka shrine known as Kawakare shrine in Gika, Minakuchi.jpg|Great cedar === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} {{commons|Category:Yasaka-jinja (Gika, Koka)}} {{DEFAULTSORT:やさかしんしや こうかし}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Yasaka Shrine (Q72183108)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Yasaka Shrine (Q72183108)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Yasaka Shrine (Q72183108)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:八坂神社]] [[Category:近江国の式内社]] [[Category:滋賀県の旧村社]] [[Category:滋賀県の神社]] [[Category:甲賀市の建築物]] [[Category:甲賀市の歴史]] [[Category:甲賀市の重要文化財]] [[Category:重要文化財 (建造物)]] [[Category:滋賀県内の市町村指定有形文化財]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QYasaka Shrine (Q72183108)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QYasaka Shrine (Q72183108)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Yasaka Shrine (Q72183108)}}[[Category:qqq]]

== Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 1160005002792 == Commons category (P373) == * Yasaka-jinja (Gika, Koka) == said to be the same as (P460) == * {{ill|Kahakareno Shrine|lt=Kahakareno Shrine|WD=Q135039798|1=Kahakareno Shrine}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '滋賀県甲賀市水口町嶬峨1606', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '滋賀県甲賀市甲賀町油日', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Yasaka-jinja (Gika, Koka)}} [[JA:八坂神社 (甲賀市)]] {{wikidata link|Q72183108}} == Japanese content == {{神社 | 名称=Yasaka Shrine | 画像=[[File:Yasaka shrine known as Kawakare shrine in Gika, Minakuchi 09.jpg|270px]]
Shrine building | 所在地={{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kōka|Q847584|WD=Q847584|1=Kōka, Shiga}}Minakuchi-chō Gika 1606 | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|57|2.125|N|136|11|48.537|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | 祭神={{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}、{{ill|lt=Emperor Shōmu|Q349263|WD=Q349263|1=Emperor Shōmu}}、{{ill|lt=Emperor Jinmu|Q200188|WD=Q200188|1=Emperor Jimmu}} | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} candidate shrine, former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} | 創建=(trad.) {{ill|lt=794|Q30887|WD=Q30887|1=794}}|例祭={{ill|lt=May 1|Q2544|WD=Q2544|1=May 1}} Kawakare Festival | 本殿=Ikken-sha {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} | 神事={{ill|lt=January 7|Q2228|WD=Q2228|1=January 7}} Matoyama no Okonai | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Shiga|label=Yasaka Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Yasaka Shrine''' (やさかじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Gika, Minakuchi-chō, {{ill|lt=Kōka|Q847584|WD=Q847584|1=Kōka, Shiga}}, {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}. === History === According to shrine tradition, Kawakare deity, ancestral deity of local development, was invited here. In {{ill|lt=794|Q30887|WD=Q30887|1=794}} (approximately {{ill|lt=Tenpyō|Q1199879|WD=Q1199879|1=Tenpyō}} 21), when Left Minister {{ill|lt=Tachibana no Moroe|Q3135863|WD=Q3135863|1=Tachibana no Moroe}} built temple {{ill|lt=garan|Q3482038|WD=Q3482038|1=Sangharama}} at neighboring {{ill|lt=Senkō-ji (Kōka)|Q72184313|WD=Q72184313|lt=Senkō-ji|1=Senkō-ji Temple (Koka, Shiga)}}, shrine buildings were built to protect Buddhist law{{Cite web|和書|title=Shrine introduction > Yasaka Shrine (Yasaka) ~ (Saga Ōmiya)|url=http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=556|accessdate=2019-09-30|publisher=Shiga Prefectural Shrine Association}}. In {{ill|lt=932|Q23656|WD=Q23656|1=932}} ({{ill|lt=Jōhei (Japan)|Q1324874|WD=Q1324874|lt=Jōhei|1=Jōhei}} 2), for distribution of divine virtue, it received the title Giga Ōmiya and shrine buildings were erected at the current site. The honden was built in {{ill|lt=1439|Q6380|WD=Q6380|1=1439}} ({{ill|lt=Eikyō|Q1193059|WD=Q1193059|1=Eikyō}} 11). According to Giga Ōmiya Senkō-ji records, the honden was rebuilt in {{ill|lt=1587|Q6744|WD=Q6744|1=1587}} ({{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 15), matching architectural style and methods. After repairs in {{ill|lt=1721|Q6913|WD=Q6913|1=1721}} ({{ill|lt=Kyōhō|Q1145048|WD=Q1145048|1=Kyōhō}} 6) and {{ill|lt=1866|Q7712|WD=Q7712|1=1866}} ({{ill|lt=Keiō|Q776775|WD=Q776775|1=Keiō}} 2), dismantling and restoration were performed in {{ill|lt=1959|Q5302|WD=Q5302|1=1959}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 34). In the {{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292|1=Heian period}} it was called Kawakare Shrine, later Gika Ōmiya, then Gozu Tennō Shrine, and now Yasaka Shrine{{Cite book|title=Kōka City History Vol.8 Kōka City Encyclopedia|date=2016-12-12|year=2016|publisher=Kōka City|page=49}}. === Enshrined deities === {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Emperor Shōmu|Q349263|WD=Q349263|1=Emperor Shōmu}}, {{ill|lt=Emperor Jinmu|Q200188|WD=Q200188|1=Emperor Jimmu}} === Festivals === * Kawakare Festival - {{ill|lt=May 1|Q2544|WD=Q2544|1=May 1}} ** Day before - first-age yakudoshi men carry lion head and visit all parishioner households ** Day of festival - rites in morning; from afternoon mikoshi procession (60 youths in Genroku-style costumes cross the Yasu River) * Matoyama no Okonai - {{ill|lt=January 7|Q2228|WD=Q2228|1=January 7}} ** As a miyaza rite, prayer for health/longevity of eldest son and family prosperity, etc. Viewing not permitted. === Precinct shrines === * Tenjin Shrine * Kumano Shrine * Kawakare Shrine * Hachiman Shrine * Yasaka Shrine === Cultural properties === === Important Cultural Property (National designation) === * Honden - ikken-sha nagare-zukuri, cypress-bark roof === City designated cultural properties === * Sacred images - made in Heian period *Gebabashi bridge - offered by parishioners in 1699 (Genroku 12) in thanks for successful rain prayer. Elaborate arched bridge. === Precinct === ファイル:Torii, Yasaka shrine known as Kawakare shrine in Gika, Minakuchi.jpg|Torii ファイル:Yasaka shrine known as Kawakare shrine in Gika, Minakuchi 03.jpg|Near stone steps ファイル:Stone bridge at Yasaka shrine known as Kawakare shrine in Gika, Minakuchi 01.jpg|Gebabashi bridge ファイル:Yasaka shrine known as Kawakare shrine in Gika, Minakuchi 04.jpg|Haiden and honden ファイル:Yasaka shrine known as Kawakare shrine in Gika, Minakuchi 07.jpg|Honden ファイル:Cryptomeria japonica at Yasaka shrine known as Kawakare shrine in Gika, Minakuchi.jpg|Great cedar === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} {{commons|Category:Yasaka-jinja (Gika, Koka)}} {{DEFAULTSORT:やさかしんしや こうかし}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Yasaka Shrine (Q72183108)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Yasaka Shrine (Q72183108)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Yasaka Shrine (Q72183108)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:八坂神社]] [[Category:近江国の式内社]] [[Category:滋賀県の旧村社]] [[Category:滋賀県の神社]] [[Category:甲賀市の建築物]] [[Category:甲賀市の歴史]] [[Category:甲賀市の重要文化財]] [[Category:重要文化財 (建造物)]] [[Category:滋賀県内の市町村指定有形文化財]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QYasaka Shrine (Q72183108)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QYasaka Shrine (Q72183108)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Yasaka Shrine (Q72183108)}}[[Category:qqq]]

== Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 1160005002792 == Commons category (P373) == * Yasaka-jinja (Gika, Koka) == said to be the same as (P460) == * {{ill|Kahakareno Shrine|lt=Kahakareno Shrine|WD=Q135039798|1=Kahakareno Shrine}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '滋賀県甲賀市水口町嶬峨1606', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '滋賀県甲賀市甲賀町油日', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Yasaka-jinja (Gika, Koka)}} [[JA:八坂神社 (甲賀市)]] {{wikidata link|Q72183108}} == Japanese content == {{神社 | 名称=Yasaka Shrine | 画像=[[File:Yasaka shrine known as Kawakare shrine in Gika, Minakuchi 09.jpg|270px]]
Shrine building | 所在地={{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kōka|Q847584|WD=Q847584|1=Kōka, Shiga}}Minakuchi-chō Gika 1606 | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|57|2.125|N|136|11|48.537|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | 祭神={{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}、{{ill|lt=Emperor Shōmu|Q349263|WD=Q349263|1=Emperor Shōmu}}、{{ill|lt=Emperor Jinmu|Q200188|WD=Q200188|1=Emperor Jimmu}} | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} candidate shrine, former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} | 創建=(trad.) {{ill|lt=794|Q30887|WD=Q30887|1=794}}|例祭={{ill|lt=May 1|Q2544|WD=Q2544|1=May 1}} Kawakare Festival | 本殿=Ikken-sha {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} | 神事={{ill|lt=January 7|Q2228|WD=Q2228|1=January 7}} Matoyama no Okonai | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Shiga|label=Yasaka Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Yasaka Shrine''' (やさかじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Gika, Minakuchi-chō, {{ill|lt=Kōka|Q847584|WD=Q847584|1=Kōka, Shiga}}, {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}. === History === According to shrine tradition, Kawakare deity, ancestral deity of local development, was invited here. In {{ill|lt=794|Q30887|WD=Q30887|1=794}} (approximately {{ill|lt=Tenpyō|Q1199879|WD=Q1199879|1=Tenpyō}} 21), when Left Minister {{ill|lt=Tachibana no Moroe|Q3135863|WD=Q3135863|1=Tachibana no Moroe}} built temple {{ill|lt=garan|Q3482038|WD=Q3482038|1=Sangharama}} at neighboring {{ill|lt=Senkō-ji (Kōka)|Q72184313|WD=Q72184313|lt=Senkō-ji|1=Senkō-ji Temple (Koka, Shiga)}}, shrine buildings were built to protect Buddhist law{{Cite web|和書|title=Shrine introduction > Yasaka Shrine (Yasaka) ~ (Saga Ōmiya)|url=http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=556|accessdate=2019-09-30|publisher=Shiga Prefectural Shrine Association}}. In {{ill|lt=932|Q23656|WD=Q23656|1=932}} ({{ill|lt=Jōhei (Japan)|Q1324874|WD=Q1324874|lt=Jōhei|1=Jōhei}} 2), for distribution of divine virtue, it received the title Giga Ōmiya and shrine buildings were erected at the current site. The honden was built in {{ill|lt=1439|Q6380|WD=Q6380|1=1439}} ({{ill|lt=Eikyō|Q1193059|WD=Q1193059|1=Eikyō}} 11). According to Giga Ōmiya Senkō-ji records, the honden was rebuilt in {{ill|lt=1587|Q6744|WD=Q6744|1=1587}} ({{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 15), matching architectural style and methods. After repairs in {{ill|lt=1721|Q6913|WD=Q6913|1=1721}} ({{ill|lt=Kyōhō|Q1145048|WD=Q1145048|1=Kyōhō}} 6) and {{ill|lt=1866|Q7712|WD=Q7712|1=1866}} ({{ill|lt=Keiō|Q776775|WD=Q776775|1=Keiō}} 2), dismantling and restoration were performed in {{ill|lt=1959|Q5302|WD=Q5302|1=1959}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 34). In the {{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292|1=Heian period}} it was called Kawakare Shrine, later Gika Ōmiya, then Gozu Tennō Shrine, and now Yasaka Shrine{{Cite book|title=Kōka City History Vol.8 Kōka City Encyclopedia|date=2016-12-12|year=2016|publisher=Kōka City|page=49}}. === Enshrined deities === {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Emperor Shōmu|Q349263|WD=Q349263|1=Emperor Shōmu}}, {{ill|lt=Emperor Jinmu|Q200188|WD=Q200188|1=Emperor Jimmu}} === Festivals === * Kawakare Festival - {{ill|lt=May 1|Q2544|WD=Q2544|1=May 1}} ** Day before - first-age yakudoshi men carry lion head and visit all parishioner households ** Day of festival - rites in morning; from afternoon mikoshi procession (60 youths in Genroku-style costumes cross the Yasu River) * Matoyama no Okonai - {{ill|lt=January 7|Q2228|WD=Q2228|1=January 7}} ** As a miyaza rite, prayer for health/longevity of eldest son and family prosperity, etc. Viewing not permitted. === Precinct shrines === * Tenjin Shrine * Kumano Shrine * Kawakare Shrine * Hachiman Shrine * Yasaka Shrine === Cultural properties === === Important Cultural Property (National designation) === * Honden - ikken-sha nagare-zukuri, cypress-bark roof === City designated cultural properties === * Sacred images - made in Heian period *Gebabashi bridge - offered by parishioners in 1699 (Genroku 12) in thanks for successful rain prayer. Elaborate arched bridge. === Precinct === ファイル:Torii, Yasaka shrine known as Kawakare shrine in Gika, Minakuchi.jpg|Torii ファイル:Yasaka shrine known as Kawakare shrine in Gika, Minakuchi 03.jpg|Near stone steps ファイル:Stone bridge at Yasaka shrine known as Kawakare shrine in Gika, Minakuchi 01.jpg|Gebabashi bridge ファイル:Yasaka shrine known as Kawakare shrine in Gika, Minakuchi 04.jpg|Haiden and honden ファイル:Yasaka shrine known as Kawakare shrine in Gika, Minakuchi 07.jpg|Honden ファイル:Cryptomeria japonica at Yasaka shrine known as Kawakare shrine in Gika, Minakuchi.jpg|Great cedar === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} {{commons|Category:Yasaka-jinja (Gika, Koka)}} {{DEFAULTSORT:やさかしんしや こうかし}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Yasaka Shrine (Q72183108)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Yasaka Shrine (Q72183108)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Yasaka Shrine (Q72183108)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:八坂神社]] [[Category:近江国の式内社]] [[Category:滋賀県の旧村社]] [[Category:滋賀県の神社]] [[Category:甲賀市の建築物]] [[Category:甲賀市の歴史]] [[Category:甲賀市の重要文化財]] [[Category:重要文化財 (建造物)]] [[Category:滋賀県内の市町村指定有形文化財]] {{DEFAULTSORT:QYasaka Shrine (Q72183108)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QYasaka Shrine (Q72183108)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Yasaka Shrine (Q72183108)}}[[Category:qqq]]

== Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 1160005002792 == Commons category (P373) == * Yasaka-jinja (Gika, Koka) == said to be the same as (P460) == * {{ill|Kahakareno Shrine|lt=Kahakareno Shrine|WD=Q135039798|1=Kahakareno Shrine}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '滋賀県甲賀市水口町嶬峨1606', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '滋賀県甲賀市甲賀町油日', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Yasaka-jinja (Gika, Koka)}} [[JA:八坂神社 (甲賀市)]] {{wikidata link|Q72183108}} == Japanese content == {{神社 | 名称=Yasaka Shrine | 画像=[[File:Yasaka shrine known as Kawakare shrine in Gika, Minakuchi 09.jpg|270px]]
Shrine building | 所在地={{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kōka|Q847584|WD=Q847584|1=Kōka, Shiga}}Minakuchi-chō Gika 1606 | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|57|2.125|N|136|11|48.537|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | 祭神={{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}、{{ill|lt=Emperor Shōmu|Q349263|WD=Q349263|1=Emperor Shōmu}}、{{ill|lt=Emperor Jinmu|Q200188|WD=Q200188|1=Emperor Jimmu}} | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} candidate shrine, former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} | 創建=(trad.) {{ill|lt=794|Q30887|WD=Q30887|1=794}}|例祭={{ill|lt=May 1|Q2544|WD=Q2544|1=May 1}} Kawakare Festival | 本殿=Ikken-sha {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} | 神事={{ill|lt=January 7|Q2228|WD=Q2228|1=January 7}} Matoyama no Okonai | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Shiga|label=Yasaka Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Yasaka Shrine''' (やさかじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Gika, Minakuchi-chō, {{ill|lt=Kōka|Q847584|WD=Q847584|1=Kōka, Shiga}}, {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}. === History === According to shrine tradition, Kawakare deity, ancestral deity of local development, was invited here. In {{ill|lt=794|Q30887|WD=Q30887|1=794}} (approximately {{ill|lt=Tenpyō|Q1199879|WD=Q1199879|1=Tenpyō}} 21), when Left Minister {{ill|lt=Tachibana no Moroe|Q3135863|WD=Q3135863|1=Tachibana no Moroe}} built temple {{ill|lt=garan|Q3482038|WD=Q3482038|1=Sangharama}} at neighboring {{ill|lt=Senkō-ji (Kōka)|Q72184313|WD=Q72184313|lt=Senkō-ji|1=Senkō-ji Temple (Koka, Shiga)}}, shrine buildings were built to protect Buddhist law{{Cite web|和書|title=Shrine introduction > Yasaka Shrine (Yasaka) ~ (Saga Ōmiya)|url=http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=556|accessdate=2019-09-30|publisher=Shiga Prefectural Shrine Association}}. In {{ill|lt=932|Q23656|WD=Q23656|1=932}} ({{ill|lt=Jōhei (Japan)|Q1324874|WD=Q1324874|lt=Jōhei|1=Jōhei}} 2), for distribution of divine virtue, it received the title Giga Ōmiya and shrine buildings were erected at the current site. The honden was built in {{ill|lt=1439|Q6380|WD=Q6380|1=1439}} ({{ill|lt=Eikyō|Q1193059|WD=Q1193059|1=Eikyō}} 11). According to Giga Ōmiya Senkō-ji records, the honden was rebuilt in {{ill|lt=1587|Q6744|WD=Q6744|1=1587}} ({{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 15), matching architectural style and methods. After repairs in {{ill|lt=1721|Q6913|WD=Q6913|1=1721}} ({{ill|lt=Kyōhō|Q1145048|WD=Q1145048|1=Kyōhō}} 6) and {{ill|lt=1866|Q7712|WD=Q7712|1=1866}} ({{ill|lt=Keiō|Q776775|WD=Q776775|1=Keiō}} 2), dismantling and restoration were performed in {{ill|lt=1959|Q5302|WD=Q5302|1=1959}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 34). In the {{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292|1=Heian period}} it was called Kawakare Shrine, later Gika Ōmiya, then Gozu Tennō Shrine, and now Yasaka Shrine{{Cite book|title=Kōka City History Vol.8 Kōka City Encyclopedia|date=2016-12-12|year=2016|publisher=Kōka City|page=49}}. === Enshrined deities === {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Emperor Shōmu|Q349263|WD=Q349263|1=Emperor Shōmu}}, {{ill|lt=Emperor Jinmu|Q200188|WD=Q200188|1=Emperor Jimmu}} === Festivals === * Kawakare Festival - {{ill|lt=May 1|Q2544|WD=Q2544|1=May 1}} ** Day before - first-age yakudoshi men carry lion head and visit all parishioner households ** Day of festival - rites in morning; from afternoon mikoshi procession (60 youths in Genroku-style costumes cross the Yasu River) * Matoyama no Okonai - {{ill|lt=January 7|Q2228|WD=Q2228|1=January 7}} ** As a miyaza rite, prayer for health/longevity of eldest son and family prosperity, etc. Viewing not permitted. === Precinct shrines === * Tenjin Shrine * Kumano Shrine * Kawakare Shrine * Hachiman Shrine * Yasaka Shrine === Cultural properties === === Important Cultural Property (National designation) === * Honden - ikken-sha nagare-zukuri, cypress-bark roof === City designated cultural properties === * Sacred images - made in Heian period *Gebabashi bridge - offered by parishioners in 1699 (Genroku 12) in thanks for successful rain prayer. Elaborate arched bridge. === Precinct === ファイル:Torii, Yasaka shrine known as Kawakare shrine in Gika, Minakuchi.jpg|Torii ファイル:Yasaka shrine known as Kawakare shrine in Gika, Minakuchi 03.jpg|Near stone steps ファイル:Stone bridge at Yasaka shrine known as Kawakare shrine in Gika, Minakuchi 01.jpg|Gebabashi bridge ファイル:Yasaka shrine known as Kawakare shrine in Gika, Minakuchi 04.jpg|Haiden and honden ファイル:Yasaka shrine known as Kawakare shrine in Gika, Minakuchi 07.jpg|Honden ファイル:Cryptomeria japonica at Yasaka shrine known as Kawakare shrine in Gika, Minakuchi.jpg|Great cedar === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} {{commons|Category:Yasaka-jinja (Gika, Koka)}} {{DEFAULTSORT:やさかしんしや こうかし}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Yasaka Shrine (Q72183108)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Yasaka Shrine (Q72183108)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Yasaka Shrine (Q72183108)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:八坂神社]] [[Category:近江国の式内社]] [[Category:滋賀県の旧村社]] [[Category:滋賀県の神社]] [[Category:甲賀市の建築物]] [[Category:甲賀市の歴史]] [[Category:甲賀市の重要文化財]] [[Category:重要文化財 (建造物)]] [[Category:滋賀県内の市町村指定有形文化財]] {{DEFAULTSORT:QYasaka Shrine (Q72183108)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QYasaka Shrine (Q72183108)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Yasaka Shrine (Q72183108)}}[[Category:qqq]]

== Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 1160005002792 == Commons category (P373) == * Yasaka-jinja (Gika, Koka) == said to be the same as (P460) == * {{ill|Kahakareno Shrine|lt=Kahakareno Shrine|WD=Q135039798|1=Kahakareno Shrine}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '滋賀県甲賀市水口町嶬峨1606', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '滋賀県甲賀市甲賀町油日', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Yasaka-jinja (Gika, Koka)}} [[JA:八坂神社 (甲賀市)]] {{wikidata link|Q72183108}} == Japanese content == {{神社 | 名称=Yasaka Shrine | 画像=[[File:Yasaka shrine known as Kawakare shrine in Gika, Minakuchi 09.jpg|270px]]
Shrine building | 所在地={{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kōka|Q847584|WD=Q847584|1=Kōka, Shiga}}Minakuchi-chō Gika 1606 | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|57|2.125|N|136|11|48.537|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | 祭神={{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}、{{ill|lt=Emperor Shōmu|Q349263|WD=Q349263|1=Emperor Shōmu}}、{{ill|lt=Emperor Jinmu|Q200188|WD=Q200188|1=Emperor Jimmu}} | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} candidate shrine, former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} | 創建=(trad.) {{ill|lt=794|Q30887|WD=Q30887|1=794}}|例祭={{ill|lt=May 1|Q2544|WD=Q2544|1=May 1}} Kawakare Festival | 本殿=Ikken-sha {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} | 神事={{ill|lt=January 7|Q2228|WD=Q2228|1=January 7}} Matoyama no Okonai | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Shiga|label=Yasaka Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Yasaka Shrine''' (やさかじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Gika, Minakuchi-chō, {{ill|lt=Kōka|Q847584|WD=Q847584|1=Kōka, Shiga}}, {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}. === History === According to shrine tradition, Kawakare deity, ancestral deity of local development, was invited here. In {{ill|lt=794|Q30887|WD=Q30887|1=794}} (approximately {{ill|lt=Tenpyō|Q1199879|WD=Q1199879|1=Tenpyō}} 21), when Left Minister {{ill|lt=Tachibana no Moroe|Q3135863|WD=Q3135863|1=Tachibana no Moroe}} built temple {{ill|lt=garan|Q3482038|WD=Q3482038|1=Sangharama}} at neighboring {{ill|lt=Senkō-ji (Kōka)|Q72184313|WD=Q72184313|lt=Senkō-ji|1=Senkō-ji Temple (Koka, Shiga)}}, shrine buildings were built to protect Buddhist law{{Cite web|和書|title=Shrine introduction > Yasaka Shrine (Yasaka) ~ (Saga Ōmiya)|url=http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=556|accessdate=2019-09-30|publisher=Shiga Prefectural Shrine Association}}. In {{ill|lt=932|Q23656|WD=Q23656|1=932}} ({{ill|lt=Jōhei (Japan)|Q1324874|WD=Q1324874|lt=Jōhei|1=Jōhei}} 2), for distribution of divine virtue, it received the title Giga Ōmiya and shrine buildings were erected at the current site. The honden was built in {{ill|lt=1439|Q6380|WD=Q6380|1=1439}} ({{ill|lt=Eikyō|Q1193059|WD=Q1193059|1=Eikyō}} 11). According to Giga Ōmiya Senkō-ji records, the honden was rebuilt in {{ill|lt=1587|Q6744|WD=Q6744|1=1587}} ({{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 15), matching architectural style and methods. After repairs in {{ill|lt=1721|Q6913|WD=Q6913|1=1721}} ({{ill|lt=Kyōhō|Q1145048|WD=Q1145048|1=Kyōhō}} 6) and {{ill|lt=1866|Q7712|WD=Q7712|1=1866}} ({{ill|lt=Keiō|Q776775|WD=Q776775|1=Keiō}} 2), dismantling and restoration were performed in {{ill|lt=1959|Q5302|WD=Q5302|1=1959}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 34). In the {{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292|1=Heian period}} it was called Kawakare Shrine, later Gika Ōmiya, then Gozu Tennō Shrine, and now Yasaka Shrine{{Cite book|title=Kōka City History Vol.8 Kōka City Encyclopedia|date=2016-12-12|year=2016|publisher=Kōka City|page=49}}. === Enshrined deities === {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Emperor Shōmu|Q349263|WD=Q349263|1=Emperor Shōmu}}, {{ill|lt=Emperor Jinmu|Q200188|WD=Q200188|1=Emperor Jimmu}} === Festivals === * Kawakare Festival - {{ill|lt=May 1|Q2544|WD=Q2544|1=May 1}} ** Day before - first-age yakudoshi men carry lion head and visit all parishioner households ** Day of festival - rites in morning; from afternoon mikoshi procession (60 youths in Genroku-style costumes cross the Yasu River) * Matoyama no Okonai - {{ill|lt=January 7|Q2228|WD=Q2228|1=January 7}} ** As a miyaza rite, prayer for health/longevity of eldest son and family prosperity, etc. Viewing not permitted. === Precinct shrines === * Tenjin Shrine * Kumano Shrine * Kawakare Shrine * Hachiman Shrine * Yasaka Shrine === Cultural properties === === Important Cultural Property (National designation) === * Honden - ikken-sha nagare-zukuri, cypress-bark roof === City designated cultural properties === * Sacred images - made in Heian period *Gebabashi bridge - offered by parishioners in 1699 (Genroku 12) in thanks for successful rain prayer. Elaborate arched bridge. === Precinct === ファイル:Torii, Yasaka shrine known as Kawakare shrine in Gika, Minakuchi.jpg|Torii ファイル:Yasaka shrine known as Kawakare shrine in Gika, Minakuchi 03.jpg|Near stone steps ファイル:Stone bridge at Yasaka shrine known as Kawakare shrine in Gika, Minakuchi 01.jpg|Gebabashi bridge ファイル:Yasaka shrine known as Kawakare shrine in Gika, Minakuchi 04.jpg|Haiden and honden ファイル:Yasaka shrine known as Kawakare shrine in Gika, Minakuchi 07.jpg|Honden ファイル:Cryptomeria japonica at Yasaka shrine known as Kawakare shrine in Gika, Minakuchi.jpg|Great cedar === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} {{commons|Category:Yasaka-jinja (Gika, Koka)}} {{DEFAULTSORT:やさかしんしや こうかし}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Yasaka Shrine (Q72183108)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Yasaka Shrine (Q72183108)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Yasaka Shrine (Q72183108)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:八坂神社]] [[Category:近江国の式内社]] [[Category:滋賀県の旧村社]] [[Category:滋賀県の神社]] [[Category:甲賀市の建築物]] [[Category:甲賀市の歴史]] [[Category:甲賀市の重要文化財]] [[Category:重要文化財 (建造物)]] [[Category:滋賀県内の市町村指定有形文化財]] {{DEFAULTSORT:QYasaka Shrine (Q72183108)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QYasaka Shrine (Q72183108)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Yasaka Shrine (Q72183108)}}

category|Category:Yasaka-jinja (Gika, Koka)}} [[JA:八坂神社 (甲賀市)]] {{wikidata link|Q72183108}} == Japanese content == {{神社 | 名称=Yasaka Shrine | 画像=[[File:Yasaka shrine known as Kawakare shrine in Gika, Minakuchi 09.jpg|270px]]
Shrine building | 所在地={{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kōka|Q847584|WD=Q847584|1=Kōka, Shiga}}Minakuchi-chō Gika 1606 | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|57|2.125|N|136|11|48.537|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | 祭神={{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}、{{ill|lt=Emperor Shōmu|Q349263|WD=Q349263|1=Emperor Shōmu}}、{{ill|lt=Emperor Jinmu|Q200188|WD=Q200188|1=Emperor Jimmu}} | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} candidate shrine, former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} | 創建=(trad.) {{ill|lt=794|Q30887|WD=Q30887|1=794}}|例祭={{ill|lt=May 1|Q2544|WD=Q2544|1=May 1}} Kawakare Festival | 本殿=Ikken-sha {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} | 神事={{ill|lt=January 7|Q2228|WD=Q2228|1=January 7}} Matoyama no Okonai | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Shiga|label=Yasaka Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Yasaka Shrine''' (やさかじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Gika, Minakuchi-chō, {{ill|lt=Kōka|Q847584|WD=Q847584|1=Kōka, Shiga}}, {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}. === History === According to shrine tradition, Kawakare deity, ancestral deity of local development, was invited here. In {{ill|lt=794|Q30887|WD=Q30887|1=794}} (approximately {{ill|lt=Tenpyō|Q1199879|WD=Q1199879|1=Tenpyō}} 21), when Left Minister {{ill|lt=Tachibana no Moroe|Q3135863|WD=Q3135863|1=Tachibana no Moroe}} built temple {{ill|lt=garan|Q3482038|WD=Q3482038|1=Sangharama}} at neighboring {{ill|lt=Senkō-ji (Kōka)|Q72184313|WD=Q72184313|lt=Senkō-ji|1=Senkō-ji Temple (Koka, Shiga)}}, shrine buildings were built to protect Buddhist law{{Cite web|和書|title=Shrine introduction > Yasaka Shrine (Yasaka) ~ (Saga Ōmiya)|url=http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=556|accessdate=2019-09-30|publisher=Shiga Prefectural Shrine Association}}. In {{ill|lt=932|Q23656|WD=Q23656|1=932}} ({{ill|lt=Jōhei (Japan)|Q1324874|WD=Q1324874|lt=Jōhei|1=Jōhei}} 2), for distribution of divine virtue, it received the title Giga Ōmiya and shrine buildings were erected at the current site. The honden was built in {{ill|lt=1439|Q6380|WD=Q6380|1=1439}} ({{ill|lt=Eikyō|Q1193059|WD=Q1193059|1=Eikyō}} 11). According to Giga Ōmiya Senkō-ji records, the honden was rebuilt in {{ill|lt=1587|Q6744|WD=Q6744|1=1587}} ({{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 15), matching architectural style and methods. After repairs in {{ill|lt=1721|Q6913|WD=Q6913|1=1721}} ({{ill|lt=Kyōhō|Q1145048|WD=Q1145048|1=Kyōhō}} 6) and {{ill|lt=1866|Q7712|WD=Q7712|1=1866}} ({{ill|lt=Keiō|Q776775|WD=Q776775|1=Keiō}} 2), dismantling and restoration were performed in {{ill|lt=1959|Q5302|WD=Q5302|1=1959}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 34). In the {{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292|1=Heian period}} it was called Kawakare Shrine, later Gika Ōmiya, then Gozu Tennō Shrine, and now Yasaka Shrine{{Cite book|title=Kōka City History Vol.8 Kōka City Encyclopedia|date=2016-12-12|year=2016|publisher=Kōka City|page=49}}. === Enshrined deities === {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Emperor Shōmu|Q349263|WD=Q349263|1=Emperor Shōmu}}, {{ill|lt=Emperor Jinmu|Q200188|WD=Q200188|1=Emperor Jimmu}} === Festivals === * Kawakare Festival - {{ill|lt=May 1|Q2544|WD=Q2544|1=May 1}} ** Day before - first-age yakudoshi men carry lion head and visit all parishioner households ** Day of festival - rites in morning; from afternoon mikoshi procession (60 youths in Genroku-style costumes cross the Yasu River) * Matoyama no Okonai - {{ill|lt=January 7|Q2228|WD=Q2228|1=January 7}} ** As a miyaza rite, prayer for health/longevity of eldest son and family prosperity, etc. Viewing not permitted. === Precinct shrines === * Tenjin Shrine * Kumano Shrine * Kawakare Shrine * Hachiman Shrine * Yasaka Shrine === Cultural properties === === Important Cultural Property (National designation) === * Honden - ikken-sha nagare-zukuri, cypress-bark roof === City designated cultural properties === * Sacred images - made in Heian period *Gebabashi bridge - offered by parishioners in 1699 (Genroku 12) in thanks for successful rain prayer. Elaborate arched bridge. === Precinct === ファイル:Torii, Yasaka shrine known as Kawakare shrine in Gika, Minakuchi.jpg|Torii ファイル:Yasaka shrine known as Kawakare shrine in Gika, Minakuchi 03.jpg|Near stone steps ファイル:Stone bridge at Yasaka shrine known as Kawakare shrine in Gika, Minakuchi 01.jpg|Gebabashi bridge ファイル:Yasaka shrine known as Kawakare shrine in Gika, Minakuchi 04.jpg|Haiden and honden ファイル:Yasaka shrine known as Kawakare shrine in Gika, Minakuchi 07.jpg|Honden ファイル:Cryptomeria japonica at Yasaka shrine known as Kawakare shrine in Gika, Minakuchi.jpg|Great cedar === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} {{commons|Category:Yasaka-jinja (Gika, Koka)}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Yasaka Shrine (Q72183108)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Yasaka Shrine (Q72183108)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Yasaka Shrine (Q72183108)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:八坂神社]] [[Category:近江国の式内社]] [[Category:滋賀県の旧村社]] [[Category:滋賀県の神社]] [[Category:甲賀市の建築物]] [[Category:甲賀市の歴史]] [[Category:甲賀市の重要文化財]] [[Category:重要文化財 (建造物)]] [[Category:滋賀県内の市町村指定有形文化財]]

Minakuchi 09.jpg|270px]]
Shrine building | 所在地={{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kōka|Q847584|WD=Q847584|1=Kōka, Shiga}}Minakuchi-chō Gika 1606 | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|57|2.125|N|136|11|48.537|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | 祭神={{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}、{{ill|lt=Emperor Shōmu|Q349263|WD=Q349263|1=Emperor Shōmu}}、{{ill|lt=Emperor Jinmu|Q200188|WD=Q200188|1=Emperor Jimmu}} | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} candidate shrine, former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} | 創建=(trad.) {{ill|lt=794|Q30887|WD=Q30887|1=794}}|例祭={{ill|lt=May 1|Q2544|WD=Q2544|1=May 1}} Kawakare Festival | 本殿=Ikken-sha {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} | 神事={{ill|lt=January 7|Q2228|WD=Q2228|1=January 7}} Matoyama no Okonai | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Shiga|label=Yasaka Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Yasaka Shrine''' (やさかじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Gika, Minakuchi-chō, {{ill|lt=Kōka|Q847584|WD=Q847584|1=Kōka, Shiga}}, {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}. === History === According to shrine tradition, Kawakare deity, ancestral deity of local development, was invited here. In {{ill|lt=794|Q30887|WD=Q30887|1=794}} (approximately {{ill|lt=Tenpyō|Q1199879|WD=Q1199879|1=Tenpyō}} 21), when Left Minister {{ill|lt=Tachibana no Moroe|Q3135863|WD=Q3135863|1=Tachibana no Moroe}} built temple {{ill|lt=garan|Q3482038|WD=Q3482038|1=Sangharama}} at neighboring {{ill|lt=Senkō-ji (Kōka)|Q72184313|WD=Q72184313|lt=Senkō-ji|1=Senkō-ji Temple (Koka, Shiga)}}, shrine buildings were built to protect Buddhist law{{Cite web|和書|title=Shrine introduction > Yasaka Shrine (Yasaka) ~ (Saga Ōmiya)|url=http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=556|accessdate=2019-09-30|publisher=Shiga Prefectural Shrine Association}}. In {{ill|lt=932|Q23656|WD=Q23656|1=932}} ({{ill|lt=Jōhei (Japan)|Q1324874|WD=Q1324874|lt=Jōhei|1=Jōhei}} 2), for distribution of divine virtue, it received the title Giga Ōmiya and shrine buildings were erected at the current site. The honden was built in {{ill|lt=1439|Q6380|WD=Q6380|1=1439}} ({{ill|lt=Eikyō|Q1193059|WD=Q1193059|1=Eikyō}} 11). According to Giga Ōmiya Senkō-ji records, the honden was rebuilt in {{ill|lt=1587|Q6744|WD=Q6744|1=1587}} ({{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 15), matching architectural style and methods. After repairs in {{ill|lt=1721|Q6913|WD=Q6913|1=1721}} ({{ill|lt=Kyōhō|Q1145048|WD=Q1145048|1=Kyōhō}} 6) and {{ill|lt=1866|Q7712|WD=Q7712|1=1866}} ({{ill|lt=Keiō|Q776775|WD=Q776775|1=Keiō}} 2), dismantling and restoration were performed in {{ill|lt=1959|Q5302|WD=Q5302|1=1959}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 34). In the {{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292|1=Heian period}} it was called Kawakare Shrine, later Gika Ōmiya, then Gozu Tennō Shrine, and now Yasaka Shrine{{Cite book|title=Kōka City History Vol.8 Kōka City Encyclopedia|date=2016-12-12|year=2016|publisher=Kōka City|page=49}}. === Enshrined deities === {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Emperor Shōmu|Q349263|WD=Q349263|1=Emperor Shōmu}}, {{ill|lt=Emperor Jinmu|Q200188|WD=Q200188|1=Emperor Jimmu}} === Festivals === * Kawakare Festival - {{ill|lt=May 1|Q2544|WD=Q2544|1=May 1}} ** Day before - first-age yakudoshi men carry lion head and visit all parishioner households ** Day of festival - rites in morning; from afternoon mikoshi procession (60 youths in Genroku-style costumes cross the Yasu River) * Matoyama no Okonai - {{ill|lt=January 7|Q2228|WD=Q2228|1=January 7}} ** As a miyaza rite, prayer for health/longevity of eldest son and family prosperity, etc. Viewing not permitted. === Precinct shrines === * Tenjin Shrine * Kumano Shrine * Kawakare Shrine * Hachiman Shrine * Yasaka Shrine === Cultural properties === === Important Cultural Property (National designation) === * Honden - ikken-sha nagare-zukuri, cypress-bark roof === City designated cultural properties === * Sacred images - made in Heian period *Gebabashi bridge - offered by parishioners in 1699 (Genroku 12) in thanks for successful rain prayer. Elaborate arched bridge. === Precinct === ファイル:Torii, Yasaka shrine known as Kawakare shrine in Gika, Minakuchi.jpg|Torii ファイル:Yasaka shrine known as Kawakare shrine in Gika, Minakuchi 03.jpg|Near stone steps ファイル:Stone bridge at Yasaka shrine known as Kawakare shrine in Gika, Minakuchi 01.jpg|Gebabashi bridge ファイル:Yasaka shrine known as Kawakare shrine in Gika, Minakuchi 04.jpg|Haiden and honden ファイル:Yasaka shrine known as Kawakare shrine in Gika, Minakuchi 07.jpg|Honden ファイル:Cryptomeria japonica at Yasaka shrine known as Kawakare shrine in Gika, Minakuchi.jpg|Great cedar === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} {{commons|Category:Yasaka-jinja (Gika, Koka)}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Yasaka Shrine (Q72183108)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Yasaka Shrine (Q72183108)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Yasaka Shrine (Q72183108)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:八坂神社]] [[Category:近江国の式内社]] [[Category:滋賀県の旧村社]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:甲賀市の建築物]] [[Category:甲賀市の歴史]] [[Category:甲賀市の重要文化財]] [[Category:重要文化財 (建造物)]] [[Category:滋賀県内の市町村指定有形文化財]]

Minakuchi 09.jpg|270px]]
Shrine building | 所在地={{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kōka|Q847584|WD=Q847584|1=Kōka, Shiga}}Minakuchi-chō Gika 1606 | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|57|2.125|N|136|11|48.537|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | 祭神={{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}、{{ill|lt=Emperor Shōmu|Q349263|WD=Q349263|1=Emperor Shōmu}}、{{ill|lt=Emperor Jinmu|Q200188|WD=Q200188|1=Emperor Jimmu}} | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} candidate shrine, former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} | 創建=(trad.) {{ill|lt=794|Q30887|WD=Q30887|1=794}}|例祭={{ill|lt=May 1|Q2544|WD=Q2544|1=May 1}} Kawakare Festival | 本殿=Ikken-sha {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} | 神事={{ill|lt=January 7|Q2228|WD=Q2228|1=January 7}} Matoyama no Okonai | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Shiga|label=Yasaka Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Yasaka Shrine''' (やさかじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Gika, Minakuchi-chō, {{ill|lt=Kōka|Q847584|WD=Q847584|1=Kōka, Shiga}}, {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}. === History === According to shrine tradition, Kawakare deity, ancestral deity of local development, was invited here. In {{ill|lt=794|Q30887|WD=Q30887|1=794}} (approximately {{ill|lt=Tenpyō|Q1199879|WD=Q1199879|1=Tenpyō}} 21), when Left Minister {{ill|lt=Tachibana no Moroe|Q3135863|WD=Q3135863|1=Tachibana no Moroe}} built temple {{ill|lt=garan|Q3482038|WD=Q3482038|1=Sangharama}} at neighboring {{ill|lt=Senkō-ji (Kōka)|Q72184313|WD=Q72184313|lt=Senkō-ji|1=Senkō-ji Temple (Koka, Shiga)}}, shrine buildings were built to protect Buddhist law{{Cite web|和書|title=Shrine introduction > Yasaka Shrine (Yasaka) ~ (Saga Ōmiya)|url=http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=556|accessdate=2019-09-30|publisher=Shiga Prefectural Shrine Association}}. In {{ill|lt=932|Q23656|WD=Q23656|1=932}} ({{ill|lt=Jōhei (Japan)|Q1324874|WD=Q1324874|lt=Jōhei|1=Jōhei}} 2), for distribution of divine virtue, it received the title Giga Ōmiya and shrine buildings were erected at the current site. The honden was built in {{ill|lt=1439|Q6380|WD=Q6380|1=1439}} ({{ill|lt=Eikyō|Q1193059|WD=Q1193059|1=Eikyō}} 11). According to Giga Ōmiya Senkō-ji records, the honden was rebuilt in {{ill|lt=1587|Q6744|WD=Q6744|1=1587}} ({{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 15), matching architectural style and methods. After repairs in {{ill|lt=1721|Q6913|WD=Q6913|1=1721}} ({{ill|lt=Kyōhō|Q1145048|WD=Q1145048|1=Kyōhō}} 6) and {{ill|lt=1866|Q7712|WD=Q7712|1=1866}} ({{ill|lt=Keiō|Q776775|WD=Q776775|1=Keiō}} 2), dismantling and restoration were performed in {{ill|lt=1959|Q5302|WD=Q5302|1=1959}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 34). In the {{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292|1=Heian period}} it was called Kawakare Shrine, later Gika Ōmiya, then Gozu Tennō Shrine, and now Yasaka Shrine{{Cite book|title=Kōka City History Vol.8 Kōka City Encyclopedia|date=2016-12-12|year=2016|publisher=Kōka City|page=49}}. === Enshrined deities === {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Emperor Shōmu|Q349263|WD=Q349263|1=Emperor Shōmu}}, {{ill|lt=Emperor Jinmu|Q200188|WD=Q200188|1=Emperor Jimmu}} === Festivals === * Kawakare Festival - {{ill|lt=May 1|Q2544|WD=Q2544|1=May 1}} ** Day before - first-age yakudoshi men carry lion head and visit all parishioner households ** Day of festival - rites in morning; from afternoon mikoshi procession (60 youths in Genroku-style costumes cross the Yasu River) * Matoyama no Okonai - {{ill|lt=January 7|Q2228|WD=Q2228|1=January 7}} ** As a miyaza rite, prayer for health/longevity of eldest son and family prosperity, etc. Viewing not permitted. === Precinct shrines === * Tenjin Shrine * Kumano Shrine * Kawakare Shrine * Hachiman Shrine * Yasaka Shrine === Cultural properties === === Important Cultural Property (National designation) === * Honden - ikken-sha nagare-zukuri, cypress-bark roof === City designated cultural properties === * Sacred images - made in Heian period *Gebabashi bridge - offered by parishioners in 1699 (Genroku 12) in thanks for successful rain prayer. Elaborate arched bridge. === Precinct === ファイル:Torii, Yasaka shrine known as Kawakare shrine in Gika, Minakuchi.jpg|Torii ファイル:Yasaka shrine known as Kawakare shrine in Gika, Minakuchi 03.jpg|Near stone steps ファイル:Stone bridge at Yasaka shrine known as Kawakare shrine in Gika, Minakuchi 01.jpg|Gebabashi bridge ファイル:Yasaka shrine known as Kawakare shrine in Gika, Minakuchi 04.jpg|Haiden and honden ファイル:Yasaka shrine known as Kawakare shrine in Gika, Minakuchi 07.jpg|Honden ファイル:Cryptomeria japonica at Yasaka shrine known as Kawakare shrine in Gika, Minakuchi.jpg|Great cedar === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} {{commons|Category:Yasaka-jinja (Gika, Koka)}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Yasaka Shrine (Q72183108)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Yasaka Shrine (Q72183108)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Yasaka Shrine (Q72183108)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:八坂神社]] [[Category:近江国の式内社]] [[Category:滋賀県の旧村社]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:甲賀市の建築物]] [[Category:甲賀市の歴史]] [[Category:甲賀市の重要文化財]] [[Category:重要文化財 (建造物)]] [[Category:Municipal Tangible Cultural Properties in Shiga Prefecture]]
